binary_or_command: ANCHOR_TEST_EACH_SLEEP_SECONDS=15 bash scripts/anchor-test-each.sh (11 TS suites, isolated solana-test-validator per file)
new_findings: anchor-test-each adjudicator: aggregate anchor test 16 failures are pure validator cross-pollution (shared PDAs 12y3Uh6s..., 8SFqwq..., BqScmyjm...) when running 13 files in one ledger. Per-file isolation: ALL GREEN at 310 passing. Lombard protocol behavior is correctly verified; no protocol bug surfaced.

binary_or_command: direct read of EVM Mailbox.sol _deliverAndHandle and Solana mailbox handle_message.rs; comparison of failure semantics
new_findings: new_signal_id=SIG-XR-003-EVM-DIVERGENCE: EVM Mailbox._deliverAndHandle wraps IHandler.handlePayload in try/catch and only sets handledPayload[payloadHash]=true on success; handler revert does NOT bubble, message remains Delivered and re-attemptable. Solana mailbox.handle_message writes Handled then invoke_signed, Anchor tx atomicity rolls back Handled on recipient CPI revert. Both layers still gate literal replays via PDA init dedupe keyed on [MESSAGE_SEED, payload_hash]. Net effect: Solana requires a brand-new tx after failed gmp_receive; EVM allows retry through deliverAndHandle.

PROP-XR-EVM-006 confirmed: handler revert absorbed by Mailbox._deliverAndHandle try/catch; payload remains re-attemptable (MessageHandled re-runs handler each call); 2-test pair passes. PROP-XR-EVM-007 confirmed: AssetRouter.changeBascule(address(0)) under DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE disables Bascule.validateMint entirely; same payload, no report, mint proceeds and totalSupply grows by mint amount. Non-admin caller is rejected.

binary_or_command: ANCHOR_MOCHA_FILES=tests/ccip.ts anchor test --skip-build -- --features localnet --no-default-features (added v6.51 negative-path tests N1/N2/N3 inside 'incoming CCIP bridge operation' describe, reusing prep)
new_findings: PROP-XR-002 refined: N1 proven — mailbox.deliver does NOT gate destination_caller; only token_pool.release_or_mint does. Wrong destination_caller is stored verbatim; userTA balance untouched at mailbox layer. N2 proven — re-init at same nonce PDA reverts (ConstraintSeeds/init dedupe) confirms cross-replay safety. N3 proven — sourcePoolAddress that has no remoteChainConfig entry is accepted by offramp.init but reverts on executeOfframp, closing the cross-layer lookup chain.

new_findings: Crucible scaffold now loads mainnet-feature lombard_token_pool.so with 3 actions (type_version, derive_accounts_release_or_mint, derive_accounts_lock_or_burn). Dry-run PASS: - 2 tracked accounts - 1 programs loaded - Single iteration completed successfully - Harness validation passed. Stateful 5s smoke: 4 executions / 0 crashes / no invariant violation. Deeper CPIs (release_or_mint/lock_or_burn/mailbox) deferred to next session since they require loading bridge.so+mailbox.so+consortium.so+bascule_gmp.so into the same Crucible context with full account wiring.

Confirmed by direct source review + Rust probe: post_session_signatures indexes current_validators[index as usize] / current_weights[index as usize] / session.signed[index as usize] without bounds check. MAX_VALIDATOR_SET_SIZE=102; index >= len(current_validators) would panic in pure Rust and surface as tx failure on Solana. Reachable impact is session DoS only (no fund-loss CPI path before panic). Pure-Rust probe in programs/consortium/src/utils/post_session_signatures_probe.rs: 3 tests pass confirming the OOB pattern. Adjudication: honest-zero for fund-loss; informational DoS-only signal. Recommendation: add require!(*index < current_validators.len() as u64, ConsortiumError::ValidatorIndexOutOfBounds).
